items.title: Ticket pricing woes for travellers and World Cup fans: CBC's Marketplace Cheat Sheet
items.summary_auto: The CBC's Marketplace rounds up the consumer and health news you need from the week.

items.title: Kentucky governor says 4 dead amid flooding caused by heavy rains
items.summary_auto: Flash flood warnings were in effect Saturday for parts of Kentucky and Indiana amid heavy rainfall, according to the National Weather Service.

items.title: King Charles reveals his income tax for the 1st time, but questions remain over royal finances
items.summary_auto: There was much interest in the move by King Charles to reveal for the first time how much income tax he pays, but in ways, the numbers that emerged this past week add to the longstanding opaqueness around royal finances.

items.title: Changes are coming to auto insurance policies in Ontario. Here's what you need to know
items.summary_auto: As of July, some benefits that were previously mandatory under standard car insurance policies will be optional. This could drop the cost of premiums for customers who opt out of certain coverages, but insurance experts warn against leaving coverage exposed to risk.

items.title: As World Cup knockout stage begins, enter the psychodrama of the penalty shootout
items.summary_auto: With so much potentially at stake from a simple strike of a soccer ball, it's understandable that the science of penalty kicks has attracted an enormous amount of research. But when it comes to the intense pressure of a World Cup shootout, is the search for the perfect penalty in vain?

items.title: Why is it so much easier to ditch a prime minister in the U.K. than in Canada?
items.summary_auto: Despite having nearly identical parliamentary systems, Canada has avoided the kind of prime ministerial parade that has befallen the U.K. in the past decade. Political watchers point to the Brexit vote as the key moment that led to half a dozen leaders occupying 10 Downing Street in the 10 years since, but there are underlying factors that make it easier for U.K. MPs to ditch a leader than their Canadian counterparts.

items.title: A glacial lake in B.C. is at risk of bursting — again. How climate change has raised this risk
items.summary_auto: The threat of a glacial lake overflowing and potentially sending a torrent of meltwater and debris rushing toward homes has become an annual fear for people living near British Columbia's Place Glacier. But experts says this is an increasing risk as the effects of human-caused climate change hit Canada's glaciers hard.

items.title: French police accused of targeting Black and Arab men with discriminatory fines
items.summary_auto: According to a report released last week by Human Rights Watch, (RE)Claim and the Community Centre for Development and Solidarity, French police systematically target Black and Arab youth, conducting identity checks and issuing on-the-spot fines.

items.title: Some families live with dementia in silence. A Hamilton group is trying to change that
items.summary_auto: The Afro Canadian Caribbean Association (ACCA) of Hamilton held a conference this week focused on brain health, dementia awareness and how to take steps to care for yourself and family members. "Families don't get the support they need because they're almost embarrassed to talk about what's happening at home," says Evelyn Myrie, president of ACCA.

items.title: Hundreds march in Vancouver to oppose planned AI data centres
items.summary_auto: Hundreds of people took to the streets in Vancouver on Saturday to oppose the construction of two new AI data centres in the city.

items.title: Chuckwagon racing legend Tom Glass dies at 77
items.summary_auto: Tom Glass, a monumental figure in Western Canadian sports history, has died after suffering a stroke at 77. As "the face of chuckwagon racing," Glass dominated the sport locally and internationally during the 1980s and 1990s.

items.title: Burnaby, B.C., search and rescue team on way to Venezuela after deadly earthquakes
items.summary_auto: Seven search and rescue volunteers from Burnaby are on their way to Venezuela Saturday to help out with rescue efforts following devastating earthquakes.

items.title: Canadian Army holds sunset ceremony to deactivate 5th Division
items.summary_auto: The 5th Division of Canadian Army is transitioning with new structure and missions. A ceremony was held on Saturday in Halifax to commemorate its accomplishments in the Atlantic region.

items.title: 66 fires currently burning in the Northwest Territories, evacuation alert remains in place for Fort Simpson
items.summary_auto: "Thousands of lightning strikes" mean a very busy weekend, says N.W.T. fire. Fort Simpson fire remains main concern, as well as fire near the Alberta border that threatens the highway. High heat and the risk of dry lightning continues for the area around Wood Buffalo National Park, Fort Smith and in the Sahtú and Dehcho.
